seo.title: Website Cost and Web Design FAQ in Nova Scotia | Hatt Digital
seo.description: Straight answers for Nova Scotia businesses about website pricing, timelines, ownership, payment, and local SEO. Most sites cost $500 to $1,500.

hero.heading: Straight answers, no fluff.
hero.lead: The questions I get asked most, answered plainly. If yours is not here, just ask.

items.q: How much does a website cost?
items.a: Most Starter sites land between $500 and $800, and most Business sites land between $900 and $1,500. Bigger builds with bookings or extra pages are quoted by scope. Either way, you get one flat, all in number before work starts. The price we agree on is the price you pay.

items.q: How long does a website take?
items.a: Most Starter sites land between $500 and $800, and most Business sites land between $900 and $1,500. Bigger builds with bookings or extra pages are quoted by scope. Either way, you get one flat, all in number before work starts. The price we agree on is the price you pay.

items.q: Can you redesign my current website?
items.a: Yes — redesigns are a big part of what I do. If your current site is slow, dated, or hard to use on a phone, I’ll rebuild it into something modern that actually works, keeping what’s good and fixing what isn’t.

items.q: Do you help with Google and SEO?
items.a: Yes. Local SEO foundations are built into every site — clean page titles, location wording, proper structure, and mobile speed — so nearby customers can find you when they search. I won’t promise guaranteed rankings (no honest person can), but I set you up the right way.

items.q: Can you help with my Google Business Profile?
items.a: Yes. I’ll guide you through setting up or tidying your Google Business Profile and pointing it at your new site, so you have a better shot at showing up in the local map results when people search nearby.

items.q: Can I update the website myself later?
items.a: If you’d like to, yes. I can set the site up so you can make simple edits to text and images yourself, with a plain-English guide. Prefer to hand it off completely? An optional care plan covers updates for you.

items.q: Do I need to have all my text and images ready?
items.a: No. Just an idea of what your business does and what you want the site to achieve is enough to start. I’ll guide you on photos and wording, and I can help write the copy if you’re stuck.

items.q: Why not just use Squarespace or Wix?
items.a: Those builders are useful tools, but the tool isn’t the strategy. Most business owners don’t have the time or the design, SEO, and copywriting know-how to turn a template into a site that actually brings in leads. You could build it yourself — but if you want it done properly and built around getting contacted, that’s the part I handle.

items.q: Do I actually own my website?
items.a: Yes — 100%. Your domain, your site, and your content are all in your name. There are no fees to access your own property and nothing locking you in. It’s yours.

items.q: I’m not techy — is that a problem?
items.a: Not even a little. I handle the technical side and explain everything in plain English. You focus on running your business; I’ll take care of the website.

items.q: What happens after I contact you?
items.a: I reply within one business day with honest advice and clear next steps. We’ll have a quick, no-pressure chat about what you need, then I’ll send a flat quote and timeline. Nothing starts until you’re happy with the plan.

mini.heading: Still have a question?
mini.body: Send me a message and I’ll give you a straight answer before you commit to anything.

cta.heading: Still weighing it up? Tell me about your website.
cta.body: Share your business, your goals, and a budget you’re comfortable with, and I’ll come back with a clear, fair quote — no pressure either way.
